title: Gates Foundation Grand Challenges: AI to Accelerate Charitable Giving

body.text: ELEVATOR PITCH

P31 Labs engineers open-source cognitive infrastructure for neurodivergent minds. Founded by a 16-year DoD civilian systems engineer diagnosed AuDHD at 39, we build the structural encapsulation that biology couldn't — turning reactive phosphorus into the most stable molecule in existence. Our flagship artifact, BONDING, is a publicly deployed multiplayer chemistry game with 413 automated tests, built end-to-end by a single neurodivergent operator using AI as cognitive prosthetic. We are the existence proof that charitable-sector AI, when designed by the people it serves, accelerates rather than replaces human judgment.
WHITESPACE

No existing entity is building fully open-source, neurodivergent-designed, research-grounded, wearable hardware dedicated to autonomic regulation and cognitive sensory gating. Existing assistive-tech vendors sell closed-source devices at medical-device markups to a population that is disproportionately unemployed and uninsured. P31 Labs releases everything under AGPLv3/MIT and targets hardware under $50 BOM.
RELEVANCE TO CHALLENGE AREA

The Grand Challenges "AI to Accelerate Charitable Giving" call asks how AI can amplify the work of small charitable organizations. P31 Labs is the proof-of-concept: a single pro-se, indigent, late-diagnosed AuDHD operator incorporated a Georgia 501(c)(3), deployed a tested game to production, published six peer-reviewable research papers to Zenodo, and built a document generation engine for every legal and grant communication — all in under ninety days — using Claude and other LLMs as cognitive prosthetic. Funding this project demonstrates the pattern: give a neurodivergent operator the right AI tools, and they out-ship a conventional nonprofit team by an order of magnitude.
EXPECTED IMPACT

Direct impact in Year 1: (a) complete and publish The Buffer — a communication-processing application for neurodivergent users currently 85% complete; (b) ship Node Zero hardware prototype at sub-$50 BOM to fifteen beta users; (c) finalize a replicable "cognitive prosthetic operator" methodology documenting how neurodivergent founders can leverage AI to run lean nonprofits.

Indirect impact: every artifact we release is upstream-compatible. Other small nonprofits, particularly disability-led organizations, can fork our document-generation engine (P31 Forge) to collapse their administrative overhead by an estimated 80%.
FEASIBILITY AND METHODOLOGY

Feasibility is demonstrated, not projected. BONDING is deployed at bonding.p31ca.org with 413 passing automated tests aligned to Next Generation Science Standards. The Georgia Secretary of State accepted our Articles of Incorporation on April 3, 2026; the IRS assigned EIN 42-1888158 on April 13, 2026. Form 1023-EZ is staged. Our methodology is to operate in tight Work Control Document (WCD) cycles, each producing one shippable artifact, validated by existing automated test infrastructure.
MEASUREMENT OF SUCCESS
Artifacts shipped (public URL, AGPLv3/MIT license, automated test coverage ≥ 80%)
Beta users recruited and retained (Node Zero hardware, The Buffer)
Peer engagement (Zenodo citations, GitHub forks, academic references)
Replication: number of external nonprofits adopting P31 Forge document pipeline
SCALABILITY POTENTIAL

The P31 methodology is designed for replication. Every engineering decision is intentionally low-cost and open: Cloudflare Pages/Workers free tier for infrastructure, AGPLv3/MIT licensing, commodity ESP32-S3 hardware, and a single-operator development model that removes coordination overhead. A second neurodivergent founder can fork our stack in under a week.
RESPONSIBLE AI PRACTICES

P31 Labs treats AI as cognitive prosthetic, not autonomous agent. All AI-generated content is reviewed, tested, and attributed by the human operator before release. Our Cognitive Passport protocol (published to Zenodo, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.xxxxx) documents the accountability chain: the human operator remains the legal author of every deliverable. No AI-generated output is shipped without cryptographic finality attestation. Training data provenance is documented where applicable; we do not fine-tune on protected-class user data.
